title: Mustardy Crispy British Meat Eton Mess
description: A hearty, pescatarian-friendly Yorkshire-inspired dish made with shredded meat, crispy pastry, and a tangy mustard sauce.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry to a large rectangle, about 1/4-inch thick.
Cut the pastry into 1-inch strips, about 12-15 strips in total.
In a bowl, mix together the shredded beef, mustard, mayonnaise, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Dip each pastry strip into the hot water, coating both sides evenly.
Place a spoonful of the beef mixture onto one end of a pastry strip, leaving a 1-inch border on either side.
Fold the pastry over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the Eton mess.
Repeat with the remaining pastry strips and beef mixture.
Place the Eton messes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each mess.
Brush the tops with a little milk and bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
To reheat, bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until crispy and hot through.
